data.title: Cathay General Bancorp declares $0.34 dividend
data.description:
• Cathay General Bancorp (NASDAQ:CATY) declares $0.34/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.06%
  • Payable June 9; to shareholders of record May 29; ex-div May 29.
  • The company has now announced a dividend of $0.34 for fifteen consecutive quarters.
  • See CATY Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Nvidia plans R&D center in Shanghai to keep foothold strong - report
Japan’s economy contracts 0.2% quarter-on-quarter in Q1
data.description:
Japan's GDP contracted by 0.2% quarter-on-quarter in Q1, marking the country's first economic shrinkage in a year and coming in below forecasts for a 0.1% decline and after a 0.6% growth in Q4, flash data showed. 

On an annualized basis, the economy contracted 0.7%, worse than consensus of a 0.2% drop and a reversal from an upwardly revised 2.4% gain in Q4. It marked the first yearly fall in a year.

Separately, Japan’s industrial production increased by 0.2% month-over-month in March 2025, beating flash data of a 1.1% decline, this followed a 2.3% rise in the previous month, marking the second straight month of growth.

On a yearly basis, industrial output grew by 1.0%, picking up from a 0.1% rise in February and marking the third consecutive annual increase.

Investors are also watching developments in US-Japan trade negotiations, with Tokyo pushing to finalize a deal by June. 

The Nikkei 225 Index fell 0.4% to around 37,600 while the broader Topix Index lost 0.1% to 2,736 on Friday, with Japanese shares sliding for the third straight session. The Japanese yen appreciated toward 145 per dollar on Friday, rising for the fourth consecutive session despite weaker-than-expected economic growth figures.

More on Japan

  • Japan's manufacturing PMI revised upward to 48.7 in April

  • Japan producer prices rise more than expected to 4.2% in March

  • Japan’s unemployment rate unexpectedly drops to 2.4% in February

  • Japan's Feb industrial production exceeds expectations, retail sales fall short of forecasts
  • Japan’s core inflation rate slows to 3% in February, exceeds forecast; food prices slow slightly

data.title: TikTok-owner ByteDance sees revenue growth of about 20% in 2025 - report
data.description:
• TikTok owner ByteDance (BDNCE) is targeting 2025 revenue of $186 billion, a 20% increase from 2024, people familiar with its finances told Bloomberg.
  • The company now reports more than 4 billion monthly active users across its apps, even as advertising demand on Douyin slows and U.S. pressure to divest TikTok continues.

data.title: KULR Technology GAAP EPS of -$0.07, revenue of $2.45M
data.description:
• KULR Technology press release (NYSE:KULR): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.07.
  • Revenue of $2.45M (+40.0% Y/Y).
  • Revenue increased 40% to $2.45 million compared to $1.75 million reported in the same period last year. Product sales increased 88.7%, with revenue of approximately $1.16 million versus approximately $615 thousand in the same quarter last year.

data.title: Caliber outlines $20M preferred stock raise and targets 25% EBITDA margin as fundraising momentum builds
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: CaliberCos Inc. (CWD) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Chris Loeffler, Chief Executive Officer and Co-Founder, stated the quarter reflected "a continuation of the strategic repositioning we began in late 2024," emphasizing a narrowed focus on three core verticals: hospitality, multifamily, and multi-tenant industrial investing. The company aims to reduce exposure to long-term development activities that do not generate current rents to a maximum of 30% of its portfolio.
  • Management highlighted cost savings initiatives, with Loeffler stating, "Our objective is to obtain an annual EBITDA margin of 25% or greater on a sustainable basis."
  • Caliber introduced a new performance allocation estimate in its 10-K, reporting estimated performance allocations of $87.7 million as of March 31, 2025. Loeffler noted, "We intend to update this estimate regularly as we grow AUM and progress towards monetization events to capture these performance allocations."
  • The company announced the qualification of its Series AA cumulative redeemable preferred stock offering by the SEC, seeking to raise up to $20 million. Loeffler reported, "The offering has brought in its first investments and our team expects to raise the full $20 million in the near term."
  • Caliber launched its 1031 exchange program, intended to "offer an attractive combination of a new and growing channel for capital, matched to a channel for Caliber to acquire and manage more stable income producing assets."
  • Fundraising challenges continued, but Loeffler announced, "in the first quarter of 2025, we matched in wholesale fundraising the same total capital we raised in all of 2024 from that same channel."
  • On project updates, significant developments include Phoenix City Council approval for the Canyon project, progress on the PURE Pickleball & Padel project, and a development rights agreement to build 15 Hyatt Studios hotels with an estimated $400 million in projects.
  • Jade Leung, Chief Financial Officer, provided a quote: "Adding in the value of our performance allocations, currently estimated to be $87.7 million, has the effect of increasing Caliber's net worth or book value substantially and providing an important picture for investors to understand a component of Caliber's value as a business."

Outlook

  • Loeffler stated, "We remain focused on achieving profitability in 2025, a goal that we believe is within reach, particularly in the second half of the year."
  • Management expects the full impact of cost savings to materialize starting in Q3, and is confident that access to capital and improvements in the financing environment will support revenue-generating opportunities.
  • The company is still assessing the impact of a delayed hotel contribution on its $3 billion AUM target by the end of 2026 but believes cost streamlining will keep it on track for profitability in 2025.

Financial Results

  • Leung reported, "Total Q1 platform revenue of $3.5 million was driven by asset management revenues. This was a 25% decrease compared to the prior period, primarily driven by a decrease in active development projects, which were eight in Q1 2024 and four in the current year quarter."
  • "Total platform expenses were $6.1 million in the first quarter of 2025, a decrease of 21% compared to Q1 of the prior year, primarily due to a decrease in operating costs related to payroll and payroll-related expenses."
  • The company posted a platform-adjusted EBITDA loss for the first quarter of $1.4 million compared to a loss of $1.7 million a year ago.
  • Managed capital reached $495.2 million, a 9.1% increase year-over-year.
  • Caliber had 196 individual unsecured notes with an aggregate principal balance of $33.2 million, of which $26.1 million have matured or will mature within the next 12 months.

Q&A

  • Brendan McCarthy, Sidoti & Company: "Just wanted to talk about the Hyatt deal. Can you go into detail on how that ultimately developed. Maybe kind of walk us through the near-term and long-term financial impact of that deal." Loeffler responded that each hotel represents about $2 million in fees, plus recurring management fees, and that the agreement covers $400 million in potential projects.
  • McCarthy: "Are you able to provide any kind of insight to, I guess, on those 2026 financial targets. I know the fundraising goal there had been $750 million..." Loeffler replied, "We're still working through the timing of those targets because obviously the not closing on that transaction was not expected...we're still analyzing...we still need some more time to quantify that and really produce, I'd say, more concrete guidance on timing."
  • McCarthy asked about the wholesale distribution channel. Loeffler said, "I feel really good about the channel...having the same order flow in the first quarter of 2025 that we saw in all of last year."
  • On monetization of non-core development projects, Loeffler indicated that performance allocations from sales of these assets are already included in the $87.7 million estimate.
  • McCarthy queried on profitability outlook. Loeffler answered, "An outperformance is going to be driven primarily by an improving financing environment and an improving fundraising...and an underperformance would be...lack of decision-making from investors."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral to slightly positive, probing for details on major deals, fundraising progress, and profitability drivers, with questions focused on clarity for targets and business model evolution.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing achievements and opportunities, and was cautiously optimistic in Q&A, using conditional language such as "We hope that that momentum continues" and "we believe we are entering a window of long-term opportunity."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment improved slightly as fundraising momentum was highlighted, while management's tone remained consistently confident but acknowledged ongoing challenges.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company reiterated its focus on three core asset classes and reduced development exposure, now targeting a maximum of 30% of the portfolio, which was previously articulated as a strategic objective.
  • Progress on cost savings initiatives continued, with meaningful reductions in expenses and headcount, and a reported decrease in platform-adjusted EBITDA loss quarter-over-quarter.
  • Fundraising showed a notable improvement in the wholesale channel, with Q1 2025 matching the entire 2024 total for that channel.
  • The estimated value of performance allocations decreased slightly from $89 million at year-end 2024 to $87.7 million by March 31, 2025.
  • Management's confidence in achieving profitability in 2025 was maintained, while acknowledging uncertainty in the timing of certain AUM and fundraising targets due to external factors.
  • Analyst questions continued to focus on deal pipeline, asset monetization, and financial targets, consistent with prior quarter concerns.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged that performance allocations are estimates and subject to change if asset plans shift materially.
  • Liquidity risk remains, with $26.1 million in unsecured notes maturing within the next 12 months, though refinancing and preferred stock offerings are underway to manage these obligations.
  • Fundraising, while improved, is still challenging and dependent on broader investor sentiment and financing conditions.
  • Ongoing market headwinds in commercial real estate and the impact of not closing certain hotel contributions were cited as factors affecting growth and AUM targets.

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized that Caliber is entering a period of opportunity with a more focused business model, targeting scalable growth in three key asset classes and aiming for a 25% EBITDA margin. The company reported progress on cost savings, strengthened fundraising momentum, and new initiatives such as the $20 million preferred stock offering and the Hyatt Studios hotel development agreement. Despite ongoing market and liquidity risks, Caliber remains confident in its strategies to achieve profitability in 2025 and continues to streamline operations while seeking to capitalize on improving market conditions and investor sentiment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Wen Acquisition announces pricing of $261M IPO
data.description:
• Wen Acquisition (WENN) Thursday announced the pricing of its initial public offering of 26,100,000 units at a price of $10.00 per unit.
  • The units are expected to be listed on The Nasdaq Global Stock Market and begin trading on May 16, under the ticker symbol “WENNU.”
  • Each unit consists of one Class A ordinary share and one-half of one redeemable warrant, each whole warrant entitling the holder thereof to purchase one Class A ordinary share at a price of $11.50 per share.

data.title: Navigator targets higher Q2 terminal throughput and expands $50M buyback amid strong Q1 cash flows
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Navigator Holdings Ltd. (NVGS) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Mads Zacho reported record quarterly revenue for Q1 2025, citing a 13% increase compared to the same period last year, driven by high utilization and higher rates. He stated, "Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 was $73 million, in line with both same period of 2024 and also Q4. The balance sheet is strong with a robust cash position even after investments into three second-hand vessels and further investments -- installments paid into the MGC newbuildings."
  • Zacho highlighted the recent $40 million bond tap and a $300 million refinancing, emphasizing a substantially stronger cash balance for Q2. He noted, "The $300 million refinancing was signed as planned in the middle of the most volatile trade environment that we've seen in decades. And this is at the lowest margins ever for Navigator."
  • Shareholder returns were reinforced with a $0.05 dividend and a new $50 million share repurchase authorization. Zacho announced, "We are likely to sell more of our older tonnage."
  • Commercially, Zacho shared, "We pushed TCE rate back up higher and secured average Q1 TCE rates of $30,475... utilization above 92%, in line with our guidance and higher than both Q4 of '24 and higher than the same period last year."
  • CFO Gary Chapman stated, "This leads on to a record high quarterly net operating revenue of $151 million, adjusted EBITDA of $72.8 million in the first quarter of 2025. Utilization was up 92.4%, up 3.1% compared to first quarter 2024."
  • Chapman noted an increase in voyage expenses due to fleet size and pass-through costs, and reported a net income attributable to stockholders of $27 million, with basic earnings per share of $0.39. He added, "Our balance sheet... continues to be strong with a cash, cash equivalents and restricted cash balance of $139 million at March 31, 2025."
  • Chapman referenced a $2.3 million unrealized loss on non-designated derivative instruments and $4.8 million other income related to a legal settlement.
  • Chief Commercial Officer Oeyvind Lindeman described freight market stability despite tariff disruptions and noted, "Rates for our core segments, ethylene and semi-refrigerated vessels, which cover 88% of our fleet, have held firm."
  • Lindeman said, "As of today, 41% of our ship days over the next 12 months are fixed at an average rate of $31,040 per day."
  • EVP Randall Giveans detailed capital returns, "During the first quarter, we repurchased more than 136,000 common shares... totaling $1.9 million... returning 25% of net income or a total of $6.8 million to shareholders during the second quarter."

Outlook

  • Zacho stated, "Near term we expect the terminal throughput for Q2 to be materially higher than Q1 and with a widening ethylene arbitrage."
  • Chapman provided that the "estimated all-in cash breakeven for 2025, which at $20,600 per day is significantly below our average TCE revenue for this first quarter of 2025 of $30,476 per day and is materially unchanged from the estimate we provided on our last earnings call back in March."
  • Lindeman said, "Utilization is now recovering, and we expect a more normalized trading pattern from May onwards."
  • Giveans relayed that a new $50 million share repurchase program is authorized and will be implemented "in the near future, especially at the current very cheap share price."

Financial Results

  • Chapman cited a net operating revenue of $151 million and adjusted EBITDA of $72.8 million for Q1 2025, with 92.4% utilization and an average TCE rate of $30,476 per day, the highest in almost a decade.
  • Vessel operating expenses were $47 million, and general and admin costs for Q1 were $8.1 million, down from Q4 2024. Other income of $4.8 million was due to a legal settlement. The quarter included a $0.9 million loss from the Ethylene Export Terminal JV due to low throughput of 85,553 tons.
  • Cash, cash equivalents, and restricted cash balance was $139 million at quarter-end, with liquidity to be further boosted by bond tap, vessel sale, and refinancing.
  • Net debt-to-adjusted EBITDA was 2.6x, and net debt to capitalization 38% at quarter-end.

Q&A

  • Unidentified Participant asked about cargo opportunities during the China-U.S. trade standstill. Lindeman responded, "We actually did some trades that we haven't done before, LPG on a handysize from Middle East to China... generally, that was a positive for the market generally for LPG."
  • On TCE rates sustainability, Lindeman said, "First quarter, most of it went Transatlantic to Europe, so on ethane and ethylene. Shorter voyages, but now we're seeing also voyages going to Indonesia and other places or longer. So yes, we are optimistic."
  • Charles Fratt (Alliance Global Partners) inquired about buyback program deployment. Giveans explained, "In terms of the scale, there are certain parameters and volume limitations that you can buy back on any given day... it is something we plan on implementing in the near term."
  • Fratt also asked if recent market volatility changed chartering strategy. Lindeman said, "That 41% is probably a little bit low. So we're looking to nudge it up a few percentage points."
  • Climent Molins (Value Investor's Edge) questioned the TCE increase source. Lindeman replied, "Mostly on the time charter market... The spot market was getting a little bit choppy leading into April."
  • Molins asked about co-payments on the Ethylene Export Terminal. Zacho said, "The deficiency payments tend to vary from contract to contract... you’ll see a benefit or a positive impact from it."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised questions on trade disruptions, chartering strategy, and buyback execution, often probing for details on how market volatility and tariffs were absorbed, reflecting a slightly positive to neutral tone.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing resilience, flexibility, and robust financial health. Zacho described the quarter as "better-than-expected" and highlighted "good cash returns."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted from cautious optimism to more pronounced confidence, particularly regarding recovery in utilization, the impact of refinancing, and expanded capital returns.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance on vessel utilization and TCE rates for Q2 remains strong, with expectations for higher terminal throughput versus Q1.
  • Average TCE rates increased from $28,341 in Q4 2024 to $30,476 in Q1 2025, with utilization up from 92.2% to 92.4%.
  • Net income attributable to stockholders rose from $21.6 million in Q4 2024 to $27 million in Q1 2025.
  • Management introduced a new $50 million share buyback authorization compared to the previous program, and vessel acquisitions and sales continued as part of fleet renewal.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on chartering, terminal throughput, and capital allocation, but current analyst tone was more focused on tactical responses to recent trade volatility and capital deployment.

Risks and Concerns

  • Zacho noted that the U.S. port fees announced "will not affect Navigator Gas negatively due to our vessel size and due to us being a service provider to U.S. energy exports."
  • Chapman reported a $2.3 million unrealized loss on derivatives and highlighted low throughput at the Ethylene Export Terminal as a temporary issue, with expectations for recovery.
  • Lindeman identified April tariff shocks as a challenge but observed a rapid market recovery as tariffs were reduced.
  • Management cited geopolitical uncertainties but pointed to a diversified customer base and strong balance sheet as mitigation factors.

Final Takeaway

Navigator Holdings Ltd. delivered a record quarter for revenue and net income, supported by robust utilization, higher TCE rates, and disciplined cost controls. Management underscored the company’s resilience in the face of recent market volatility and trade uncertainty, while reaffirming its commitment to shareholder returns through an expanded $50 million buyback. Near-term outlook is buoyed by expectations of a materially higher Q2 terminal throughput and normalization of trading activity, with ongoing fleet renewal and consistent capital allocation strategies remaining central to Navigator’s approach for sustained growth and value creation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Kohl’s announces pricing of private offering of $360 million senior secured notes
data.description:
• Kohl’s (NYSE:KSS) Thursday announced the pricing of its private offering of $360 million aggregate principal amount of 10.000% senior secured notes due 2030.
  • The Notes will be guaranteed by Kohl’s subsidiaries.
  • Certain guarantees will be secured by, among other collateral, 11 distribution centers and e-commerce fulfillment facilities, which will be held in a newly-formed holding company.
  • The offering is expected to close on May 30.
  • Kohl’s (NYSE:KSS) intends to use the net proceeds from the sale of the Notes in a series of transactions resulting in the repayment of borrowings under its revolving credit facility.
  • Kohl’s expects to borrow under its revolving credit facility to repay all of its 4.25% notes due 2025 at maturity.
